behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/LLM-DEP: the failure is often a real embedding-model contract mismatch (wrong model/version or provider change), which a stubbed vector hides. Correctness here depends on actual model output shape from a real AI/ML call, not just local parsing logic. MULTI-STEP is not required (single model step). Rejected higher level (Workflow): behavior text checks one embedding output property only, not a chained multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding model configured in production for a fixed query, then assert returned vector length equals the live index dimension configured for the target vector collection.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is a module-boundary behavior (search service + vector DB adapter/index), not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is inherently required if tested against an in-process/local test index. LLM-DEP: no model judgment quality is needed. Rejected higher levels: System is disqualified because behavior text is strictly score ordering semantics, reproducible with local index; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI decision content is under test.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a local test vector index with known vectors, issue a query vector, and assert returned items are sorted strictly by descending cosine score (including tie handling expectations).

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: this explicitly tests model judgment ('nuanced intent' and reordered relevance), which stubs cannot validate. MOCK-HIDE: mocked reranker can always force reorder and miss true failures where the real model does not apply nuanced intent. MULTI-STEP: only one agentic decision boundary is essential here, so not Workflow. Rejected higher level (Workflow): behavior text targets a single reranking decision, not multiple chained agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run real retrieval to get raw top-K, invoke the real LLM reranker on nuanced benchmark queries, and assert at least one position change occurs in cases where human-labeled intent requires contextual reordering.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure typically appears at handoff between similarity scores and threshold/filter policy (retrieval module + business rule), so above Unit. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with a local seeded index and deterministic threshold config; no mandatory real network/OS behavior. LLM-DEP: behavior is about empty-vs-noise gating, not model judgment quality. Rejected higher levels: Agentic is disqualified because behavior text does not require evaluating LLM output quality; Workflow is disqualified because no multi-agent chain is required.
behaviors.test_description: Seed an index with vectors intentionally distant from a test query vector and configure production relevance threshold; assert API returns an empty list (not low-score items) before/after rerank stage as applicable.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA + MULTI-STEP: this is an end-to-end SLO across the full live pipeline (API ingress, embedding call, vector search, LLM rerank, response). In-process fakes hide real latency bottlenecks and tail behavior. LLM-DEP is present because real model/API latency materially affects pass/fail, and multiple model-involved stages are sequenced. Rejected lower levels: Integration/System alone are insufficient because they do not require the full multi-step agentic pipeline under realistic conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like load test against the real API path with real embedding and reranking dependencies, collect latency distribution, and assert median (p50) end-to-end latency remains under 2.0s over a representative query set.
